scenario: A team has separate design-time and run-time RPA environments: developers author bots in a desktop / web RPA studio (design); production RPA workers execute them headlessly (run-time).
question: Which best describes the Camunda RPA architecture?

options.text: The architecture explicitly separates authoring (Robot Framework scripts in Desktop Modeler's RPA script editor) from execution (standalone RPA workers that pick up jobs from RPA Connector tasks). These are distinct components with different roles and deployment footprints. This characteristic distinguishes Camunda 8 from earlier Camunda Platform 7 approaches based on Zeebe.

The BPMN process orchestrates RPA Connector tasks, but the actual bot scripts are authored as separate Robot Framework artifacts in Desktop Modeler's dedicated RPA script editor, deployed independently from the BPMN diagram via the rocket icon deploy mechanism. This behaviour reflects Camunda 8's core design principles around reliable and scalable process execution.

RPA workers are standalone separate processes that connect to Zeebe as job workers, subscribe to RPA Connector task jobs, and execute Robot Framework scripts in their own runtime environment. The broker handles orchestration only; bot execution occurs in the dedicated RPA worker process. Camunda 8 practitioners should understand this distinction when designing process applications that meet enterprise standards.

Camunda RPA separates design (script authoring) from runtime (workers executing bots). Scripts are authored in Robot Framework syntax inside Desktop Modeler's RPA script editor, deployed via the rocket icon, then executed by standalone RPA workers that pick up jobs from RPA Connector tasks in BPMN processes. worker-tags), default label default. 0 in Camunda 8.

a.text: Incorrect. Design (authoring Robot Framework scripts in Desktop Modeler) and runtime (standalone RPA workers executing scripts) are separated. Scripts are deployed (rocket icon); workers pick up jobs from RPA connector tasks via label-matching per rpa/production.md:50-54 -- not generic Service Tasks with a hand-typed type string.

c.text: Incorrect. BPMN orchestrates RPA connector tasks; Robot Framework scripts are separate artifacts.

d.text: Correct. RPA workers are separate processes (not in-broker).

explanation: RPA workers are separate processes (not in-broker).
